creator: anatoly.borodin@gmail.com
text:
Hi!

On Sat, Mar 7, 2009 at 12:50, Yar Odin <yarodin@gmail.com> wrote:
> There are two workarounds for this problem:
>
> 1. Downgrade gnupg to 2.0.9 =C2=A0(i'm choose this variant)
>
> 2. Upgrade KGpg to 2.0/2.1 (and KDE to 4.1/4.2 of course)

As I use KGPG from KDE3, I just install gnupg1 first, so gpg=3Dgpg1. But
one day I will want to switch to KDE4 and gnupg2, how could I dump and
restore the keys database? Could you provide a script or something?
Thanks in advance!


--=20
Mit freundlichen Gr=C3=BC=C3=9Fen,
Anatoly Borodin
